top_categories.workout_context: 13,878ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Maratona dles Dolomites' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
At mile 60 of 85, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
162ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Maratona dles Dolomites. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 85 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Maratona dles Dolomites' alpine mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 85 miles.
At Maratona dles Dolomites, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on alpine mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 13,878ft of climbing on alpine mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 85 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Maratona dles Dolomites, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Maratona dles Dolomites’s 85 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Maratona dles Dolomites with 13,878ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Summits above 2,200m can feel near-freezing in rain.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 85 miles of alpine mountain passes in La Villa → Corvara, Alta Badia, South Tyrol.
